name: Raspberry-Rose Geranium Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Rose geranium leaves, washed and patted dry
1 (or 2) fresh geranium leaves for final bottling
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the vinegar in a nonreactive saucepan until hot but not boiling.
2. Place the raspberries in a wide-mouthed quart (or larger) jar and crush lightly with a wooden spoon.
3. Pour in the hot vinegar and geranium leaves.
4. Cover the jar with plastic wrap and secure with string or a rubber band.
5. Season for at least 2 weeks in a cool, dimly lit place, occasionally swirling the contents.
6. Strain the vinegar through a sieve lined with 2 layers of dampened cheesecloth into a sterilized bottle.
7. Add the fresh raspberries and fresh geranium leaf to the bottle.
8. Cap or cork the bottle.
